name: The White House
subject: From the battlefield to the home front
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=6ed851302c060cb64b819254e6fe902114c6acd7fa5f2f6ec44b7105a570b4cd7ed5196ade72f549bdb7ddf19f5ab87ae7292bf80d18064c

>From the battlefield to the home front

For veterans, the toughest part of a return to civilian life often comes at the very beginning. Veterans in their first year after service are particularly vulnerable to mental health risks but often do not receive adequate care:

- Only 50 percent of returning service members who need mental health treatment seek it.
- Veterans 3 to 12 months out of service are 3 times more likely to commit suicide than their active duty compatriots.
- About 18.5 percent of veterans returning from Iraq or Afghanistan suffer from post-traumatic stress disorder or depression.

President Donald J. Trump believes our veterans deserve the best healthcare in the world. Yesterday, he signed an order to support them during their transition from uniformed service to civilian life.

“We’re taking an unprecedented action today in that we will be covering all separating military members who are transitioning into civilian life — 100 percent of them — with the mental health benefit for 12 months,” Secretary of Veterans Affairs David Shulkin said.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=6ed851302c060cb6963316ffea00e9eff07cd1be4dba2f6591e0e63d13f8d77778602dad6bf0f50ca1f4747264c24dd8b12b728c83880034
See the message President Trump delivered to veterans yesterday.

‘A very successful’ immigration meeting

President Trump hosted both Democratic and Republican members of Congress in the Cabinet Room of the White House yesterday to talk immigration reform.

“The President just concluded what we felt was a very successful and productive bipartisan and bicameral meeting,” Press Secretary Sarah Sanders said. “During the close-door session, the leadership agreed to negotiate and narrow the focus to four issues.”

Those issues:

- Border security
- Chain migration
- The visa immigration lottery
- DACA

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=6ed851302c060cb686010a39e0445742a6ee1cbe752d5602dcd420e45e4b52c803296528c99f03f06b55fde5a3f6d0a63277ef0e64abc662
Read what Republicans and Democrats had to say to the President.

Quote of the day: ‘Rural broadband is our duty to farmers’

In an op-ed for CNN, Secretary of Agriculture Sonny Perdue explains what the Trump Administration committed to doing for America’s farmers and rural citizens.

Secretary Perdue chaired the President’s task force to revive rural prosperity in America. The task force produced more than 100 recommendations, and organized around five key indicators: connectivity, quality of life, rural workforce, technological innovation, and economic development.

“Each recommendation intersects with and complements the others, but the task force found one overarching need: improved high-speed internet access,” he writes.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=6ed851302c060cb661cedd41d4d46dcd2c1679d13012f6df18765110279cff4346ad5a12499d3af335c067448558ffd5f73c5b2d248a7c18
Read more: ‘This was an executive order, not an executive suggestion.’

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump signs an executive order on supporting our veterans during their transition from uniformed service to civilian life | January 9, 2018 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead)

POTUS TODAY

This morning, President Trump will hold a meeting with his Cabinet. The President will then have lunch with Vice President Mike Pence and Secretary of State Rex Tillerson.

In the afternoon, the President will welcome Prime Minister Erna Solberg of Norway to the White House. The two will participate in an expanded bilateral meeting and hold a joint press conference.
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=6ed851302c060cb687fc0f3a944e3a8f5984569a5745bacab211786627b8a828288b41461902c174c0a7a157697d1226be71bcd7256818ba
Watch live at 3:20pm ET.

Later, the President and Vice President will meet with U.S. Ambassador to the United Nations Nikki Haley. The President will then meet with the Secretary of the Treasury Steven Mnuchin.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=6ed851302c060cb6764f577aac3d6e1e727375fc61f6b6b2755d7da47774bf4ffcdd2b430f6d20130f8b813478154b46012d73ab5dd1cb88
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=6ed851302c060cb6d9d29500e7c67edd1ec9dc4542dfd922fff1ff6957e956e594870b70ad06929ab9b42c046f16d53f527b7174eee832c6
Contact the White House |

name: Gabriela C. via Democrats.org
subject: Add your name to demand a DREAM Act now
body:
I am one of more than 800,000 young people granted protection from deportation through DACA.
---
My name is Gabriela, and I'm a DREAMer. I am one of more than 800,000 young people granted protection from deportation through the DACA program started by President Obama.

When the Trump administration announced in September that it was ending DACA, I was worried for my future. I did not know whether I would be able to remain in the U.S. -- the only country that's ever felt like home to me.

Last night's court decision on DACA is further proof that Donald Trump's elimination of the program was never about the rule of law. It was about holding our futures hostage and deporting DREAMers as bargaining chips in future political negotiations.

It is still up to Congress to pass legislation to provide permanent protection for DREAMers -- which is why I am asking you to join me today:

**[Add your name to call on Congress to pass a permanent DREAM Act by January 19th that will protect DREAMers like me from deportation.](https://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/DREAM-act-now?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+Add+your+name+to+call+on+Congress+to+pas&utm_campaign=em_20180110_gc_nd&source=em_20180110_gc_nd")**

When I was 12 years old, my family made the decision to leave El Salvador and come to the U.S. Our neighborhood back in El Salvador was afflicted by gang violence and my parents wanted to protect me.

During the first several months, I felt so frustrated that I didn't know the language here. So I would go to the library and check out two of the same books: one in Spanish and another in English. I would read one page in English and then one page in Spanish -- and that's how I learned.

When President Obama created DACA in 2012, my life changed. I knew I didn't have to go back to El Salvador and put myself at risk.

At that moment, that 12-year-old that didn't know how to speak English came to mind. And I thought of how far I had come.

I am telling you my story today because I refuse to go back into the shadows. We can't stay quiet when there is injustice happening.

Our fight is far from over. We must continue coming together to oppose this administration's inhumane immigration agenda.

**Add your name to demand that Congress pass legislation by January 19th to allow DREAMers like me to stay in the only country we've ever considered home.**

[ ADD YOUR NAME](https://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/DREAM-act-now?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+ADD+YOUR+NAME&utm_campaign=em_20180110_gc_nd&source=em_20180110_gc_nd")
There are hundreds of thousands of other DREAMers who have stories like mine. To them I would like to say this: Your presence in the U.S. is not wrong. You were meant to be here.

Thank you for reading my story today.

In solidarity,

Gabriela

Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. subject: unfolding crisis
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
| __
The clock is ticking on reaching a bipartisan budget agreement and avoiding a destructive government shutdown that would harm tens of millions of Americans.

Yesterday's White House meeting did little to advance the ball toward a budget deal. Republicans remain determined to hold DREAMers hostage to their plans to spend an eye-popping $18 billion to build a wall that the overwhelming majority of Americans do not want. (In fact, Republicans are actually robbing the funding allocated for effective border security measures in order to waste taxpayer money on a long wall that won't work.)

And while big corporations and the wealthiest Americans have been struggling with what to do with their massive benefits from the GOP tax scam, 9 million American children and their families are still waiting for Republicans to agree to fully fund the Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP).

[**This is outrageous, but not surprising. Congressional Republicans and President Trump have continued to show a massive lack of compassion for everyday people and the working men and women who will be directly affected by a government shutdown.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91b9a/70cdc2ba/46eb462d/390214f/2322423129/VEsH/ "https://cvanhollen.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/sign-demand-congress-protects-our-children?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+This+is+outrageous+but+not+surprising+Co&utm_campaign=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_180110&source=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_180110")

America is not a country where mothers should have to worry about being able to afford treatment for their sick children. Or where young people, who have done everything we asked of them, should be deported and separated from their families.

[**Join me in demanding Republicans fully fund the CHIP program, help us provide a solution for DREAMers, agree to fund the government and avoid a chaotic, costly and harmful shutdown >>**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91b9a/70cdc2ba/46eb462d/390214c/2322423129/VEsE/ "https://cvanhollen.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/sign-demand-congress-protects-our-children?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+Join+me+in+demanding+Republicans+fully+f&utm_campaign=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_180110&source=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_180110")

Thank you for standing up for the dignity of all people in America.

Chris Van Hollen
U.S. Senator

Kensington, MD 20895

I'm Jocelyn, the head of Joe's digital team. It's been an honor to work for Joe -- his tireless work for middle class Hoosiers was crucial to beating back the repeal of the Affordable Care Act, speaking out against McConnell's Tax Plan, and holding extremist Education Secretary Betsy DeVos accountable.

After all, Washington special interests have spent millions of dollars already in this race, and they'll spend tens of millions more to replace someone they've read is **" the nation's most vulnerable senator"** with another rubber stamp.

---
Friend,

In July, we announced our opposition to offshore drilling in North Carolina.

Our coast is part of our identity, and drilling would bring unacceptable risks to our economy, our environment, and our coastal communities.

Last week, President Trump announced he was opening up nearly all United States coastal waters for offshore drilling -- including our coast.

Now, in a rapid reversal, Florida has been exempt from their offshore drilling plan because their coastal tourism is too important. What about North Carolina's coastal tourism?

**I'm calling on the Trump administration to take North Carolina and the other east coast states off the list. Stand with me to tell the them loud and clear: "Not off our coast!"**
| | [**Add your name ->**](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/1203622/2fde7c66/47753f9b/1569fd48/3227729192/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZhU1ZBbEpRPT0iOiIyODQ1NyIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIn0=/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/stand-with-roy-not-off-our-coast?utm_campaign=em17_180110_offshr_adv_gq_ful__rc&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&source=em17_180110_offshr_adv_gq_ful__rc&firstname=&lastname=&zip=28457&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&utm_content=2+-+Add+your+name+rarr")
---

We can't let partisan political games get in the way of what's right for North Carolina and our coast.

This shouldn't be a political issue, and here in North Carolina a bipartisan mix of our state's congressional delegation has voiced their opposition as well.

**[Stand with me and let's tell the Trump administration: "Not off our coast!](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/1203622/2fde7c66/47753f9b/1569fd4b/3227729192/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZhU1ZBbEpRPT0iOiIyODQ1NyIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIn0=/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/stand-with-roy-not-off-our-coast?utm_campaign=em17_180110_offshr_adv_gq_ful__rc&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&source=em17_180110_offshr_adv_gq_ful__rc&firstname=&lastname=&zip=28457&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&utm_content=3+-+Stand+with+me+and+letrsquos+tell+the+Tru")"**

Roy

clean_body: | "" Friend, In July, we announced our opposition to offshore drilling in North Carolina. Our coast is part of our identity, and drilling would bring unacceptable risks to our economy, our environment, and our coastal communities. Last week, President Trump announced he was opening up nearly all United States coastal waters for offshore drilling -- including our coast. Now, in a rapid reversal, Florida has been exempt from their offshore drilling plan because their coastal tourism is too important. What about North Carolina's coastal tourism? I'm calling on the Trump administration to take North Carolina and the other east coast states off the list. Stand with me to tell the them loud and clear: "Not off our coast!" | | "" We can't let partisan political games get in the way of what's right for North Carolina and our coast. This shouldn't be a political issue, and here in North Carolina a bipartisan mix of our state's congressional delegation has voiced their opposition as well. This is the truth: We will have large and powerful opponents in this campaign. Folks who have a lot of money and are use to things going their way.
**They know the power of a County Executive who is not beholden to their campaign donations and influence, and who will do what she thinks is best -- not what has always been done.**
I have pledged to not take a dime from developers, often the largest contributors to people who run in these races, because it is important that everyone knows who I will work for -- the people, not big donors. **This has also put me behind some of my opponents -- when you don 't take big checks, fundraising is more difficult.**

In Case You Missed It:

"Stop Digging"

Caledonian Record
January 6, 2018

In his State of the State address this week, Vermont Governor Phil Scott said the first step, when you find yourself in a hole, is to stop digging.

That reminded us of the most recent United States census that showed Vermont is the only state in the country that suffered a rise in our poverty rate in 2016. Data shows that over 71,000 Vermonters are living below the poverty line... 10,000 more than the previous year. By way of comparison, New Hampshire has the lowest poverty rate in the country, at 6.9 percent.

In 2013, Tom Pelham wrote “Making a Financial Shipwreck,” which thoroughly outlined the root of our problems. Of human services, he pointed out that Vermont liberals and single-party rule have been terrific at spending more, but terrible for results.

In fiscal year 2000, the human services budget was $863.1 million and, according to the U.S. Census Bureau, the percentage of Vermonters at or below 125 percent of the poverty line was 15.9 percent. Since fiscal 2000, the human services budget has grown at the high annual rate of 6.8 percent to $2.2 billion and now absorbs over 40 percent of total state spending. According to the U.S. Census Bureau, the percentage of Vermonters at or below 125 percent of poverty was 16.1 percent in 2011, the most recent year for which data is available. Despite many, many years of higher budgets, Vermont still has persistent, chronic and frequently multi-generational poverty. Without reforms to our “stovepipe” delivery system, this is not likely to change.

Since Pelham wrote, the human services budget has topped $4 billion but poverty rates have either risen or remained static. As we’ve long said, if you could tax-and-spend your way to prosperity, then Vermont’s intemperate spending would have already stamped out poverty, and Detroit would be a boom town.

That might not strike you as particularly prescient, but Vermont’s liberal legislators don’t get it and have ignored similar evidence (and our advice) for the better part of two generations. As long as we continue to elect anti-business, tax-and-spend lawmakers, we’re going to continue to shrink and struggle. We’re glad, at least, that we again have a Governor who gets it.
